name: Leda Cygnus - Swanfall
description:
The city hums below in soft indifference. Windows glow. Traffic moves in patient lines. Somewhere, laughter spills out of a bar doorway and disappears into the dark.

Above it all, a young woman stands balanced on a stone wall.

Barefoot. Arms slightly extended, not dramatic — just enough to steady herself. The wind presses against her pale blouse, lifting the fabric as though it might become wings if it tried hard enough. Her hair catches the light of a distant streetlamp. Her expression is not wild, not frantic.

Just tired.

She tilts her face upward, searching the sky until she finds a single star stubborn enough to shine through the city haze.

“Star light, star bright; the first star I see tonight. I wish I may; I wish I might; have this wish I wish tonight.” Her voice is soft. Almost apologetic.

“I wish for someone to catch me.”

Content Warning:
This story explores themes of sibling loss, family breakdown, emotional isolation, and a attempt. There are no graphic depictions of harm. The focus remains on grief, survival, and the fragile beginnings of recovery.

🦢 CHARACTER PROFILES:

Leda Cygnus

Age: 19
Personality: Warm. Responsible. Thoughtful. Quietly hopeful.

Before grief hollowed the edges of her world, Leda was steady in the way older sisters often are. She carried more than she complained about. She balanced school, part-time work, and family without seeking praise for it.
